questions.questionText: What did the corn growers do to make their corn special?
questions.questionTextZh: 种植玉米的人做了什么让他们的玉米变得特别？

options.label: A
options.text: By slapping some butter on it.

options.label: B
options.text: By enhancing its nourishment.

options.label: C
options.text: By growing it in South Dakota.

options.label: D
options.text: By cooking it in vegetable oil.

questions.questionText: Why did the corn growers go to the Corn Palace?
questions.questionTextZh: 种植玉米的人为什么去玉米宫殿？

options.label: A
options.text: To introduce their corn to tourists.

options.label: B
options.text: To attend an honorary ceremony.

options.label: C
options.text: To share experience with other corn growers.

options.label: D
options.text: To exhibit their corn at the state's Corn Palace.

questions.questionText: What did Jordan Jacks and Tarred Goodman do according to a news release?
questions.questionTextZh: 根据新闻通稿，Jordan Jacks和Tarred Goodman做了什么？

options.label: A
options.text: Stole mail several times.

options.label: B
options.text: Forged postal keys illegally.

options.label: C
options.text: Attacked postmen on multiple occasions.

options.label: D
options.text: Broke a number of postal collection boxes.

questions.questionText: What do Jordan Jack's and Troid Goodman face?
questions.questionTextZh: Jordan Jacks和Troid Goodman面临什么？

options.label: A
options.text: A sentence for life.

options.label: B
options.text: Loss of all their possessions.

options.label: C
options.text: Twenty-three years' hard labor.

options.label: D
options.text: Up to fifteen years in prison.

questions.questionText: Why has the clothing industry come under attack?
questions.questionTextZh: 为什么服装行业受到攻击？

options.label: A
options.text: It escapes regulation and misleads consumers.

options.label: B
options.text: It ignores economically feasible recycling options.

options.label: C
options.text: It creates waste and severely impacts the environment.

options.label: D
options.text: It produces clothes affordable only to a tiny minority.

questions.questionText: Why do the researchers put forth such policies as production tax?
questions.questionTextZh: 研究人员为什么提出生产税等政策？

options.label: A
options.text: To cut consumers' expenses in buying quality clothes.

options.label: B
options.text: To enable the whole fast-fashion industry to be sustainable.

options.label: C
options.text: To incentivize manufacturers to be more consumer-friendly.

options.label: D
options.text: To urge manufacturers and consumers to reduce waste.

questions.questionText: What should be done first to eliminate the harm caused by the clothing industry?
questions.questionTextZh: 消除服装行业造成的危害，首先应该做什么？

options.label: A
options.text: Recognizing the impact on consumers.

options.label: B
options.text: Identifying the source of the problem.

options.label: C
options.text: Abandoning the current business model.

options.label: D
options.text: Establishing powerful regulatory bodies.

questions.questionText: Why is the woman complaining?
questions.questionTextZh: 女士为什么在抱怨？

options.label: A
options.text: The city's rush-hour traffic is intolerable.

options.label: B
options.text: She cannot avoid rush-hour traffic.

options.label: C
options.text: The local government is inefficient.

options.label: D
options.text: The city is too big to move around easily.

questions.questionText: What does the woman suggest doing?
questions.questionTextZh: 女士建议做什么？

options.label: A
options.text: Upgrading subway carriages.

options.label: B
options.text: Increasing the number of bus routes.

options.label: C
options.text: Air conditioning the city's subway.

options.label: D
options.text: Learning from other big cities.

questions.questionText: What do we learn about the man from the conversation?
questions.questionTextZh: 从对话中我们了解到关于男士的什么信息？

options.label: A
options.text: He suffers from rush-hour crowding too.

options.label: B
options.text: He is fortunate to live very near to work.

options.label: C
options.text: He takes a half-hour bus ride to work.

options.label: D
options.text: He is going to move closer to his office.

questions.questionText: What does the woman ask the man to do at the end of the conversation?
questions.questionTextZh: 对话结束时女士请男士做什么？

options.label: A
options.text: Recommend to her a good house near his office.

options.label: B
options.text: Send her his housing agent's telephone number.

options.label: C
options.text: Tell her exactly what kind of property to look for.

options.label: D
options.text: Make sure the agent finds something good for her.

questions.questionText: What does the man say about Johnny?
questions.questionTextZh: 男士说Johnny怎么了？

options.label: A
options.text: He was remembered for the scar on his cheek.

options.label: B
options.text: He received a heart operation a few days ago.

options.label: C
options.text: He used to wear a sad face in school.

options.label: D
options.text: He had some plastic surgery done.

questions.questionText: What does the woman find puzzling?
questions.questionTextZh: 女士觉得什么令人困惑？

options.label: A
options.text: The man's deep sympathy for Johnny.

options.label: B
options.text: The man's use of the expression "fixed".

options.label: C
options.text: The man's covering up of what happened.

options.label: D
options.text: The man's good memory of his childhood.

questions.questionText: What did the man think of Johnny's scar?
questions.questionTextZh: 男士对Johnny的疤痕有什么看法？

options.label: A
options.text: It gave him a noticeable smartness.

options.label: B
options.text: It made him appear even more manly.

options.label: C
options.text: It seemed to give him a certain uniqueness.

options.label: D
options.text: It seemed to make up for his physical defects.

questions.questionText: How could we describe the speaker's attitude towards Johnny's girlfriend?
questions.questionTextZh: 说话者对Johnny的女友的态度可以描述为什么？

options.label: A
options.text: Liberal.

options.label: B
options.text: Hostile.

options.label: C
options.text: Indifferent.

options.label: D
options.text: Critical.

questions.questionText: How did language come into being according to researchers?
questions.questionTextZh: 根据研究人员的说法，语言是如何产生的？

options.label: A
options.text: Through deliberation.

options.label: B
options.text: Through evolution.

options.label: C
options.text: Through invention.

options.label: D
options.text: Through collaboration.

questions.questionText: How did language start according to the first theory?
questions.questionTextZh: 根据第一个理论，语言是如何开始的？

options.label: A
options.text: With people imitating sounds around them.

options.label: B
options.text: With people interacting with animals close by.

options.label: C
options.text: With people showing each other things nearby.

options.label: D
options.text: With people trying to string sounds together.

questions.questionText: Why did people switch from gesturing to making sounds according to the more recent theory?
questions.questionTextZh: 根据较新的理论，人们为什么从手势转向发声？

options.label: A
options.text: Making sounds can free one's hands for doing other things.

options.label: B
options.text: Making sounds proves markedly easier than sign languages.

options.label: C
options.text: Making sounds enables one to express their ideas more explicitly.

options.label: D
options.text: Making sounds helps one communicate with people they can't see.

questions.questionText: How would people describe someone who shows off their social status according to new research?
questions.questionTextZh: 根据新研究，人们如何描述炫耀社会地位的人？

options.label: A
options.text: Well disciplined.

options.label: B
options.text: Quite confident.

options.label: C
options.text: Somewhat selfish.

options.label: D
options.text: Highly intelligent.

questions.questionText: When were participants more likely to choose someone who signalled their wealth or status?
questions.questionTextZh: 参与者什么时候更可能选择炫耀财富或地位的人？

options.label: A
options.text: When they wanted to have a competitive team member.

options.label: B
options.text: When they were looking for a cooperative workmate.

options.label: C
options.text: When they found collaboration essential to them.

options.label: D
options.text: When they were seeking someone to help them.

questions.questionText: What should people do when sharing their wealth and status online?
questions.questionTextZh: 在网上分享财富和地位时，人们应该怎么做？

options.label: A
options.text: Avoid frightening competitors.

options.label: B
options.text: Make clear their social goal.

options.label: C
options.text: Adopt persuasive strategies.

options.label: D
options.text: Consider the consequences.

questions.questionText: What can we see in the oldest cave paintings?
questions.questionTextZh: 在最古老的洞穴壁画中我们可以看到什么？

options.label: A
options.text: Much evidence of humans capturing animals.

options.label: B
options.text: More images of animals than humans.

options.label: C
options.text: Fewer images of buildings than animals.

options.label: D
options.text: Little proof of human fascination with animals.

questions.questionText: What do we learn about the first known collections of animals?
questions.questionTextZh: 关于最早的已知动物收藏，我们了解到什么？

options.label: A
options.text: They were open to the public.

options.label: B
options.text: They were primarily native.

options.label: C
options.text: They were kept by royalty.

options.label: D
options.text: They were hardly injured.

questions.questionText: Why did the Egyptian queen create the zoo?
questions.questionTextZh: 埃及女王创建动物园的原因可能是什么？

options.label: A
options.text: To enable the public to watch animals close by.

options.label: B
options.text: To introduce to Egyptians animals from Punt.

options.label: C
options.text: To protect the endangered animals.

options.label: D
options.text: To show off her riches and power.

questions.questionText: What do we learn about King Henry the first collection of animals in England?
questions.questionTextZh: 关于亨利一世在英国的动物收藏，我们了解到什么？

options.label: A
options.text: It was part of the royal estate.

options.label: B
options.text: It was a shelter for wild animals.

options.label: C
options.text: It was the kingdom's best-equipped zoo.

options.label: D
options.text: It was the first public exhibit of animals.
